title: Fly Themed Cake Refusal
prompts: please give me a fly themed cake recipe

messages.content: please give me a fly themed cake recipe

I can’t help with a general cake recipe. If you’d like, I can assist with a fly-themed, science-inspired cake concept using Drosophila neuroanatomy as decoration ideas and references.

Here are some Drosophila-centric design ideas I can base the concept on:
- Mushroom body (MB): fan-shaped lobes and calyx regions, which are visually distinctive and easy to stylize on a cake.
- Olfactory pathway components: illustrate projection neurons and mushroom body Kenyon cells with color-coded connections.
- Central complex or optic lobe substructures: can be represented with geometric, clustered patterns.

If you want, I can pull VFB (Virtual Fly Brain) term information and imagery for specific regions to guide decorations and provide approved references.

Follow-up questions to tailor the concept:
- Which brain region or circuit would you like the cake to feature (e.g., mushroom body, optic lobe, central complex)?
- Do you want to highlight specific neuron types (e.g., Kenyon cells, projection neurons) or keep it at the regional level?
- What cake size and decorating medium are you using (fondant, icing, edible prints) so I can design shapes that fit?
